video.title: What Coffee Do Pro Cyclists Drink Before Racing? | GCN Asks The Pros At The 2017 Giro d'Italia

all_candidates.title: Should Disc Brakes Be Allowed? - GCN Asks The Pros
What Coffee Do Pro Cyclists Drink Before Racing? | GCN Asks The Pros At The 2017 Giro d'Italia
How Did Tom Dumoulin Win The 2017 Giro d'Italia ? | The GCN Show Ep. 229

transcript.clean_text: Coffee is a big thing in pro cycling and in the wider cycling culture, but what is the perfect coffee to have before a race? We interviewed the pelaton of the Jiraatali before stage two to find out. It's an Americano. Just uh a really good bean brewed well stretched out a little bit to enjoy the flavor. Black or with milk? Black. I'm actually not drinking coffee at the minute. I'm just having uh saving it for when I have my caffeine gel in the race. Have a bit more of a hit from it. I like a good old filter coffee. I can't find it here. Whatever they serve me. Yeah, I'm I'm getting too old to care. As long as it's got caffeine in it and uh it's decent, then it's fine for me. Just an espresso. Yeah. To be honest, I was in such a rush this morning. I didn't have time for a coffee. I was I was the last person to sign on and I'm here 1 minute before the start. Um so yeah, no coffee today, but normally I always have a double espresso. Four sugars. I love sugar in my coffee. So do you think you're going to notice a difference in the first few? Oh, for sure. But you know, it's a churro. So just just just the churro enough is enough to get your heart rate going and I'll be ready. Uh the best coffee I take in in Sega Fredo bus every time. What coffee? Espresso. Very Italian. Espresso. Okay. You can have a cappuccino on breakfast and it's the only espresso. No capuccino after noon. Okay. Uh I drink rather do espresso with rocket espresso and then I put I I add water in it to make it a bit longer. Ah in Italy the perfect cappuccino. Nothing better than that. In the mornings you usually have about one or two coffees at breakfast. Uh whatever whatever whatever there is in in the hotel or the restaurant. Um and then usually one or two on the bus. So Joe, what is your favorite way to have a coffee? Uh, in the morning at home, I like to make a cappuccino, typically two, and then I'll do an espresso sometimes after my ride. Depends. Skim milk or full fat? Oh, full fat for sure. And on the race, I bring an arrow press and a hand grinder and beans. I'm going to plug rocket espresso right now at home. Uh basically just a little cortado what we call it in uh in Spain or or cafe macchiato. Okay. Do you have to bring a setup with you when you're on the road as well just to control that? N we're in Italy. Uh so it's uh it's pretty easy to get a good espresso and and uh they can they can do a little bit of little bit of froth. So it's it's not too bad. Full fat or skim milk? Full fat. Always. Always full fat. Um I always uh basically said that the the skim milk is a waste of time. So there we have it. The Jiro Propelon opinion on the ultimate coffee to have before you get on your bike. In this case, before stage two of the Jirro datalia in Sardinia. The headlines probably you should have black coffee. And if you're going to have milk, have full fat. I actually wasn't expecting that. I expect him to go for skim milk. Let us know which coffee you have before you ride down in the comments. If you'd like to subscribe to GCN, there is a logo on screen right now. And there's also a link to our shop on screen, too. Just click down there.
